paper_title: Chrysophycean Cyst and Diatom Analysis in Lake Nautajärvi

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of chrysophyte cyst and diatom samples from sediment traps and surface sediment using standard diatom treatment. The purpose is to count and identify chrysophyte cysts and diatoms to understand their distribution and accumulation rates.

steps.description: Chrysophyte cyst and diatom samples from sediment traps and surface sediment were prepared using the standard treatment for diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare chrysophyte cyst and diatom samples using standard diatom treatment

steps.description: For chrysophyte cyst analysis, the aliquot suspensions were dried on stubs for scanning electron microscope (SEM) work.
steps.searchable_summary: dry aliquot suspensions on stubs for SEM analysis

steps.description: A minimum of 200 chrysophyte cysts were counted and identified from SEM images.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify chrysophyte cysts from SEM images

steps.description: Unidentified cysts were classified using probabilistic counting based on the percentage distribution of different morphotypes in a sample.
steps.searchable_summary: classify unidentified cysts using probabilistic counting

steps.description: For diatom analysis, the samples were mounted on microscope slides using Naphrax®.
steps.searchable_summary: mount diatom samples on slides with Naphrax

steps.description: A minimum of 300 diatoms were counted and identified along random transects at 1000x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify diatoms at 1000x magnification

steps.description: DVB microscopic markers were also counted to determine chrysophyte cyst and diatom accumulation rates from the sediment trap samples.
steps.searchable_summary: count DVB markers to determine accumulation rates

methods.description: This method involves the use of statistical techniques to analyze changes in species richness and abundance of chrysophyte cysts and diatoms across different seasons and years.

steps.description: Changes in species richness were analyzed using rarefaction analysis with the program PAST 2.17b.
Species abundance diagrams were created using the program C2.
steps.searchable_summary: create species abundance diagrams using C2

steps.description: Detrended correspondence analysis (DCA) was used to explore compositional variation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DCA to explore compositional variation

steps.description: Principal components analysis (PCA) was used to summarize major patterns of variation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to summarize variation patterns

steps.description: Redundancy analysis (RDA) and partial RDA were used to explore relationships between biota and environmental variables.
steps.searchable_summary: perform RDA to explore biota-environment relationships

steps.description: All species were transformed to percentage abundances and square-root transformed prior to statistical analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: transform species data to percentage and square-root
